summary: Latency-sensitive first-finish testing for Telegram alerts + Groww broker linking. All 7 backend endpoints (settings GET/PUT, telegram/test, alerts CRUD+toggle+check, groww sync) return expected status codes and payloads. Frontend Settings dialog (Telegram + Groww tabs) and Alerts panel render, wire up correctly, and integrate with backend. Alert create/list/check flow validated end-to-end with toast display. Cleanup performed for created test alerts.
passed: GET /api/settings returns configured=false with empty masked tokens on fresh state
PUT /api/settings persists telegram_bot_token + chat_id; masked value returned; telegram_configured=true
POST /api/settings/telegram/test with fake token -> 400 with Telegram 401 Unauthorized (graceful, no 500)
POST /api/alerts creates target/stop_loss/pct_change alerts; invalid type -> 400 'invalid alert_type'
GET /api/alerts lists all created alerts with correct fields
PATCH /api/alerts/{id}/toggle flips active flag (verified true->false and back)

POST /api/alerts/check evaluates against live yfinance quotes; RELIANCE stop_loss=2000 triggered (LTP ~1304 < 2000); returned {checked:2,triggered:1} initially and {checked:3,triggered:1} after re-toggle (target likely in cooldown due to already-triggered stop_loss earlier — no crash, valid response)
POST /api/broker/groww/sync -> 400 'Groww API token not configured. Add it in Settings.' when token missing
Frontend loads; alerts-panel visible with 'Auto-checks every 5 min · 9:15am–3:30pm IST · Mon–Fri' note
Settings dialog opens with tab-telegram and tab-groww; all inputs (token, chat) and buttons (save/test) present
Send-Test button correctly ENABLED when telegram_configured=true
Groww tab: token input + save + sync buttons present; sync button correctly DISABLED when token not saved
Add-alert dialog opens; symbol/threshold/note filled; submit creates alert visible in panel (TCS target ≥ ₹5000)
Check-alerts button triggers /api/alerts/check and displays toast: 'Checked 1 alerts, 0 triggered'
Existing features (Portfolio header/cards, AI Trade Agent panel with diversification/risk/insights) render unaffected

failed.issue: Sending empty-string or null telegram_bot_token/chat_id does NOT clear existing values (fields remain configured). This is by design (partial update) but there is no explicit clear endpoint. Not a blocker for user setup flow.
failed.evidence: PUT with {telegram_bot_token:null, telegram_chat_id:null} -> still returns telegram_configured=true with previous masked value

action_items: Optional: consider allowing explicit clear via empty string in PUT /api/settings, or add a DELETE endpoint for tokens. Non-blocking for first finish.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in app; no credentials needed per review request
cleanup_performed: Deleted 3 backend-created RELIANCE test alerts and 1 UI-created TCS alert. Fake telegram token remains stored (see LOW-priority finding); user's real config will overwrite it.
